notes: This file records the existence and public-safe motivation of a private diagnostic track.
It intentionally omits source code, architecture internals, training recipes, data mixtures, checkpoints, and reproduction-critical metrics.
motivation: Compact byte-level models can spend raw context faster than subword-token systems. This track tests whether compression-oriented training pressure can improve usable context efficiency while preserving base language-model behavior.
public_safe_questions: Can compact byte-level models use context more efficiently?
Can context-efficiency improve without harming held-out BPB or external language-model checks?
Does structural transfer survive broader training?
Are observed failures architectural, curricular, or compute-limited?
